summary.intent: Test the --evaluator-url mechanism end-to-end by building an HTTP wrapper server for command evaluators and wiring it into the live integration orchestrator, then validating the full RED-GREEN-OBSERVER cycle through an HTTP transport.
summary.outcome: Successfully implemented and validated the HTTP evaluator path: created evaluator_http_server.py, added --evaluator-url to live_integration.py, confirmed identical scores (0.8731) via HTTP vs command transport, fixed an incorrect model name (gpt-5.1-mini → gpt-5.1) that was causing null scores, and added EXAMPLES.md with four worked evaluator-type examples. All changes pushed to origin/main.

repo: live_integration.py only supported --evaluator-command at session start; --evaluator-url was a CLI-only feature and had no orchestrator-level support
HTTP evaluator protocol is minimal: POST {"candidate": "..."} → {"score": 0.85, ...}; the CLI does a preflight GET health check before optimization begins
gpt-5.1-mini does not exist in OpenAI's model lineup; gpt-5.1 (full) is the correct general-purpose model; gpt-5.1-codex-mini exists but rejects the temperature parameter that llm_judge.py passes
The skill_clarity.sh evaluator produces a stable baseline score of ~0.8731 for skills/generate-evaluator/SKILL.md — HTTP transport is transparent and produces identical scores to --evaluator-command
live_integration.py uses subprocess output parsing for judge scores, which loses the code-fence-stripping recovery logic present in llm_judge.py when an LLM wraps JSON in markdown

The entire/checkpoints/v1 branch contains 64 commits and 313 files (mostly .jsonl transcripts); trufflehog scan returned 0 verified secrets — all 30 findings were false-positive NpmToken hits on conversation UUIDs

code.finding: New file: stdlib http.server wrapper. Key correctness details: Content-Length must use len(bytes) not len(str) to handle non-ASCII correctly; server.server_close() must be in a finally block to prevent socket leaks on unexpected exceptions; ThreadingHTTPServer preferred over HTTPServer for socket reuse
Added --evaluator-url flag with mutual exclusion against --evaluator-command; GREEN phase passes flag to optimize CLI; RED phase passes flag to score CLI via new _score_with_url() helper
Help text example referenced gpt-5.1-mini (non-existent model); corrected to gpt-5.1
7 judge model mock references used gpt-5.1-mini; all updated to gpt-5.1

workflow: trufflehog --only-verified should be the first pass for secret scanning; NpmToken false positives from UUIDs are common in transcript/log files — cross-reference flagged values with context before acting

Subagent-driven development pattern: implementer → spec reviewer → code quality reviewer → fix → commit produces better code than single-pass implementation (caught socket leak and Content-Length bug)
When a model returns null scores in a scoring pipeline, check model name validity before debugging the transport — the model may simply not exist
gpt-5.1-codex-mini rejects temperature=0.0; use gpt-5.1 as the default OpenAI judge model for LLM-judge workflows

friction: gpt-5.1-mini does not exist — caused null/error scores in the initial RED phase run, requiring a two-iteration correction (first to gpt-5.1-codex-mini, then to gpt-5.1 when codex-mini rejected temperature)
gpt-5.1-codex-mini rejects the temperature parameter, causing a second round of model name changes after the first fix was insufficient

live_integration.py subprocess output parsing is fragile for LLM judge output — loses the recovery logic from llm_judge.py, so markdown-wrapped JSON from a judge silently produces a null score rather than recovering
trufflehog produced 30 false-positive NpmToken findings on conversation UUIDs, requiring manual verification to confirm they were benign
open_items: No new pytest unit tests cover the HTTP evaluator path — current test coverage is live-integration-only for --evaluator-url; mock-based tests for evaluator_http_server.py and the live_integration --evaluator-url flag are missing
live_integration.py subprocess parsing for judge scores should be made tolerant of markdown-wrapped JSON to match the recovery behavior in llm_judge.py
A model-id validator (suggested during session) that checks model names against litellm's known list before running long optimization jobs was not implemented
EXAMPLES.md evaluator scripts are illustrative/non-runnable stubs — they reference example files (api_error_message.txt, customer_support_prompt.md, cli_help.txt) that do not exist in the repo
Draft commit 4b6864d remains in git history as a superseded precursor to the evaluator server implementation
